front: Novel: the protagonist contracts cholera after eating overripe strawberries and collapses attempting to rise to meet a Polish boy he has been stalking
Novel: a barber helps the protagonist dye his hair and paint his face; he imagines himself as Socrates lecturing Phaedrus on beauty
Novel: the protagonist dreams of a bacchanalia-like ritual dedicated to the 'Stranger God' at the Hotel des Bains
Novel: the real-life inspiration for the boy Tadzio was Baron Wladyslaw Moes; Gustav Mahler partly inspired the protagonist Aschenbach
Novel: Tony marries the bankrupt Grunlich and the alcoholic hops merchant Alois Permaneder; the last male member Hanno dies of typhoid
Novel: Thomas Buddenbrook dies from a botched tooth extraction shortly after an emotional reaction to Schopenhauer's The World as Will and Representation
Novel: Tony's teacher is the hunchbacked crone 'Sesemi' Weichbrodt; Hanno's breakfast hot chocolate symbolizes decadence
Novel: the humanist Settembrini (student of Carducci) spars with the Jesuit Naphta (fictionalized from Gyorgy Lukacs); Naphta commits suicide during their duel
Novel: the Russian patient Clavdia Chauchat (with 'Kirghiz eyes') is the protagonist's romantic interest; he flirts with her in French on Walpurgis Night
Novel: the protagonist hallucinates while skiing in a blizzard; a recurring leitmotif is borrowing a pencil; characters begin saying 'we up here'
Novel: the composer Adrian Leverkuhn voluntarily contracts syphilis to increase his artistic output; narrated by Serenus Zeitblom; composes Apocalypsis cum Figuris
Novel: Theodor Adorno's twelve-tone explanations aided its conception, causing a feud with Schoenberg; Adorno convinced the author not to redeem the protagonist
Novel: the protagonist is embarrassed when called 'Fraulein' after an incorrect dance move; infatuated with Ingeborg Holm and Hans Hansen; Lisaveta calls him a 'lost burgher'
Short Story: an allegory of fascism about the hypnotist Cipolla, who is shot by a youth after hypnotizing him into kissing his cheek; set in Torre di Venere
Novel: Sita faces a dilemma after two men decapitate themselves before a statue of Kali and have the wrong heads placed on their bodies
Short Story: the title character's obsessive need to show kindness leads him to stab his dog Esau so he can comfort it while dying
Author: Helen Tracy Lowe-Porter translated almost all his works; chose the word 'magic' for The Magic Mountain though this author preferred 'enchanted'
Author: Colm Toibin fictionalized this author's life in the novel The Magician
